english: v. yaṅ (yang) 1.
Bhot. and Schr.
v. yáṅ-po (yang po).
the second of two final letters, viz. s (s) after g (g), ṅ (ng), b (b), m (m).
(spelling uncertain), is said to be the n. of a green stone, which is worked into handles of knives etc. W.
v. dág-pa (dag pa).

or, in B., com. pleon. after the affixed am (am) (gam (gam), nam (nam) etc.), which in itself already expresses the or; it is also preceded by daṅ (dang); further, Thgy.; either - or -, yaṅ-na (yang na) (ni (ni)) - yaṅ-nu (yang nu) (ni (ni)) -.
v. sprúl-pa (sprul pa).

Cs., *yáṅ-mo* (*yang mo*) C. and W. *yáṅ-ghe* (*yang ghe*) Ts. adj., yáṅ-ba (yang ba) adj. and sbst., light, lightness, opp. to lči-ba (lci ba), q. cf.; - fig. jam-źiṅ yáṅ-ba ('jam zhing yang ba) what is soft and light, commodious and easy Dzl.; of food cf. lči-ba (lci ba) II.; weak, *de saṅ yáṅ-mo yin* (*de sang yang mo yin*) W. this is a weaker, less emphatic, word than that; *ṅo yáṅ-mo* (*ngo yang mo*) C., W. cheerful, happy.
early barley, v. nas (nas) I.
great-grandfather, yaṅ-mes-mo (yang mes mo) great-grandmother Sch.
very high skill, consummate art Mil.
the highest point, summit, fig. the height of perfection.
great-grandson Sch.
W. buck, ram, he-goat, = pá-ra (pa ra).
prob. = yáṅ-na (yang na) S.g.
n. of a hell Thgy.

1. also -po (-po), wide, broad, large, t'aṅ (thang), sa-gźi (sa gzhi) a large or wide field, plain Glr.; yaṅs-śiṅ rgya-č'e-ba (yangs shing rgya che ba) large and spacious, of a house S.O.; *gṅ-sa* (*gng sa*) (or ṅé-sa (nge sa)) yaṅ-pa dug* (*yang pa dug*) W. here is much room; fig. *mig-yáṅ* (*mig yang*) C., W. liberal, generous, bounteous; *yaṅ-méd-la* (*yang med la*), *yaṅ-yáṅ-pa-la* (*yang yang pa la*) W. sudden, unexpected, unawares; *yaṅ-lúg čò-če* (*yang lug co ce*) W. to hang or throw a coat over, without getting into the sleeves; yaṅ-śam byéd-pa (yang sham byed pa) id., Sch.; *yaṅ-hlúb* (*yang hlub*) C., W. wide, of clothes. - 2. v. gyéṅ-ba (gyeng ba).
Ssk. वैशाली, Dzl. and elsewh., city in ancient India, now Allahabad.

(= ya (ya) II., opp. to man (man) III q. v.) what is uppermost, man-yan (man yan) below and above Cs.; yán-na (yan na) Cs.: above, in the beginning, in the first part; gen. yan (yan) stands as adv. or postp with accus., = yán-la (yan la), yan-čád(-la) (yan chad (-la)), yan-čód(-la) (yan chod (-la)), above, in the upper part, lté-ba yan stéṅ-la yód-de (lte ba yan steng la yod de) Glr. lit. above the navel standing out of (the water), i.e. standing in (the water) up to the navel; sta-zúr yan-čád (sta zur yan chad) Dzl. above the hips; lo-brgyád yan-čád (lo brgyad yan chad) Pth. above eight years old; otherwise when referring to time, always till, to; often preceded by nas (nas), from ... forth, Glr.
dulcimer, musical instrument in Ts.

adj., free, vacant, unoccupied, having no owner, of places and things that are common property, like the air, rocks and stones etc.; kyi yán-pa (kyi yan pa) a dog without a master, vagrant dog; gral yán-la yod (gral yan la yod) there are yet places unoccupied; of fields: untilled, fallow-ground; yan kyúr-la ma ča (yan kyur la ma ca), v. kyúr-ba (kyur ba); yán-gar-ba (yan gar ba) separate, apart, by itself Lis., rgyal-rigs yán-gar-ba źig (rgyal rigs yan gar ba zhig) a separate dynasty, a dynasty of its own; yán-gar-du (yan gar du) id., adv. Was. (281); rgya-yán (rgya yan) the external world, rgya-yán(-gyi) gnyén-pa (rgya yan (-gyi) gnyen pa) Glr. a helper from the external world; sems rgya-yán-du ma śór-bar byos (sems rgya yan du ma shor bar byos) take care that the mind be not distracted by outward things; *yan ča-če* (*yan ca ce*) W. to disperse, *lug (lug), nor tsáṅ-ma (nor tsang ma), sam-pa yan soṅ* (*sam pa yan song*) W., the sheep have dispersed (or a sheep has strayed), the fortune is gone, the thoughts are lost, wandering; yán-du j̇úg-pa (yan du 'jug pa) to suffer (the sheep) to disperse on the pasture; nad yán- (nad yan-)
